claim: Durable Objects (stateful serverless programming) are a true innovation: first implementation of the actor model in an infrastructure layer, spinning up millions of stateful things with serverless characteristics
actionable: Consider Durable Objects for stateful agentic systems that need persistence without managing VMs; enables efficient scaling of stateful agents
source_title: Why you should build Science Fiction — Sunil Pai, Cloudflare

claim: Dynamic Workers allow safe execution of LLM-generated code in isolated environments with zero startup time; you can expose only specific APIs and control all outgoing traffic
actionable: Use Dynamic Workers to safely execute code generated by LLMs; whitelist allowed APIs and block outgoing traffic by default for security
source_title: Why you should build Science Fiction — Sunil Pai, Cloudflare

claim: MCP with search and execute tool calls can reduce thousands of API endpoints to two tool calls by having the agent write code to search/execute within an isolate (type-checked, no back-and-forth)
actionable: Implement search and execute MCP patterns for large API surfaces; let agents write code instead of exposing every endpoint as a tool
source_title: Why you should build Science Fiction — Sunil Pai, Cloudflare

claim: No one has yet built the React for AI agents: everyone is building different harnesses but there's no standardized architecture like React unified frontend frameworks
actionable: Expect continued fragmentation in agent frameworks; design systems to be framework-agnostic where possible; consider adopting emerging standards like MCP
source_title: Why you should build Science Fiction — Sunil Pai, Cloudflare

claim: Open source repos have become adversarial environments; maintainers face fake security reports and external attacks; projects like Agents SDK shut down contributions and only allow issues
actionable: When maintaining open source agents/tools, be prepared to restrict contributions and carefully vet issues; use AI to help triage and respond
source_title: Why you should build Science Fiction — Sunil Pai, Cloudflare

claim: Forking is a fundamental sign of prestige and respect in open source culture; developers should fork and adapt code rather than rebuilding, enabling them to own their dependencies
actionable: Encourage and embrace forks of your open source work; view them as adoption rather than competition; fork important dependencies you depend on to own the supply chain
source_title: Why you should build Science Fiction — Sunil Pai, Cloudflare

claim: Build original sci-fi style projects rather than incremental improvements; don't optimize for enterprise deals; use agentic tech and LLMs to try wild, novel ideas others won't
actionable: When building agents or AI tools, prioritize originality and exploration over market sizing; take risks on speculative but feasible ideas
source_title: Why you should build Science Fiction — Sunil Pai, Cloudflare
